_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of data encryption practices and principles
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI SP domain. Knowledge SP-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-K-007 (SP, cosine: 0.571), DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.556).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.6/10 (raw 0.463). Shared vocabulary: [data, knowledge, principles]. NICE-only terms [encryption, practices] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, architecture, consent, default, design, limitation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.885 (P=0.919, R=0.853). P exceeds R by 0.066 — the NICE element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of data encryption practices and principles" and "Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including thei". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.586 > STS 0.463 (gap +0.123). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, knowledge, principles] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.8%. Shared tokens: [data, knowledge, principles]. NICE-only: [encryption, practices]. WIDAI-only: [application, architecture, consent, default, design, limitation].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% on Superset of classification. The matched KSA operates in a different professional domain than the obligation/competency requires. STS 0.463 passed the threshold but secondary signals contradict the match.
STS 0.463 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.123 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
